title: Batatas glaucifolia
taxonomy: Batatas glaucifolia is a plant species classified within the genus Batatas, which is a part of the family Convolvulaceae. The Convolvulaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as morning glories and bindweeds. The genus Batatas is a monotypic genus, containing only one known species, Batatas glaucifolia. This species is native to Madagascar and is also known as the 'Madagascar Potato'. The specific classification of Batatas glaucifolia is not well-documented, but it is believed to be a type of flowering plant that is closely related to the genus Ipomoea.
